text:
Market today: The mood in chips is cautious, despite some earnings, driven by Samsung's fall on capex and demand worries. This directly impacts your concentrated tech/chip book.

Your holdings: SOXL is down 23.52% and SPAL is down 11.17%; both are leveraged ETFs needing close attention to their stops. Remember, margin amplifies these drops. SMH is also down 5.04% amidst chip sector concerns.

Watch: Keep a tight leash on your leveraged SOXL and SPAL positions. Also, watch for any further contagion in the broader chip sector following Samsung's news.

Bottom line: Focus on managing risk in your leveraged and chip-heavy positions, especially with sector-specific headwinds.
plays: Consider trimming SOXL given Samsung news and its leveraged decay; respect your 200 stop.
Monitor SPAL closely; it's a new, leveraged position already down 11% and prone to fast moves. Respect the 21.38 stop.
No new buys today - hold and watch. The chip sector headwinds make new long entries risky, despite scanner signals.
